title: End-to-end proof: Discuss-authored automation, Vault-sealed Google email, self-heal, no leak

implement.detail: tests/z1-end-to-end.test.ts exercises the REAL chain: keychain-sealed vault (no passphrase) -> scopedSecrets delivers ONLY GOOGLE_ACCESS_TOKEN -> REAL google connector.mjs runAction sends gmail (token reaches the Authorization header) -> REAL automation engine drives a REAL chromium (reads the doc heading 'Q3 Report') -> injected recoverable failure -> REAL fixer self-heal (run_patch) -> verify -> completed. Discuss kickoff (H1) is the authoring entry. Walkthrough: evidence/z1-walkthrough.gif (asciinema of the proof + full suite).

test.detail: z1-end-to-end passes; redaction proof is MEANINGFUL — a connector result deliberately carries the token, and the test asserts the engine scrubbed it to ***REDACTED*** in BOTH the persisted record AND the SSE events while the raw token is absent from record/events/logs/vault-file. Full suite 1366 passed.

codexReview.detail: R1 (high): no-leak check was false-green (token never serialized pre-redaction) -> probe injects a token-bearing result + asserts active scrubbing; (medium) over-claimed real connector path -> prose narrowed (backend route+spawn covered separately). R2: APPROVE — redaction proof meaningful, claims match exercised path.

codexPwTest.detail: vision/operative-bound; the live browser I/O is exercised against real chromium in-test

walkthrough.detail: evidence/z1-walkthrough.gif + .cast — shows the Z1 proof passing + the full feature suite green; the assertions ARE the self-verification
